frameworks.name: active inference / expected-information-gain saccade selection
frameworks.one_line: Next fixation chosen to maximize expected gain in classification certainty.

frameworks.name: two-stream (ventral What / dorsal Where) processing
frameworks.one_line: Anatomical what/where split instantiated as foveal classifier plus peripheral saliency map.

frameworks.name: saliency map
frameworks.one_line: Peripheral 'Where' pathway computed as a saliency-style map over the visual field.

frameworks.name: signal-detection / speed-accuracy
frameworks.one_line: Model output benchmarked against human visual-search speed-accuracy structure.

core_question: Can a dual foveal-peripheral visuomotor model implement visual search as a focal accuracy-seeking policy that reproduces human eccentricity-dependent acuity and speed-accuracy tradeoffs?
key_assumptions: Target position and category are drawn independently from a common generative process.
Ventral and dorsal pathways factor inference into 'what to see' versus 'where to look'.
Saccade goal is to maximize expected gain in classification certainty.
Foveal acuity is high; peripheral acuity falls off with eccentricity.
manipulations: Pathway architecture: foveal classifier ('What') vs peripheral saliency map ('Where').
Fixation location selected by the saccade policy across the image.
key_findings: Model reproduces the speed-accuracy structure of human visual search.
Focal accuracy-seeking saccade policy yields effective target localization and categorization.
interpretations: Anatomical ventral/dorsal separation is functionally consistent with a what/where factorization of visuomotor inference.
Visual search can be cast as expected-gain-in-certainty saccade selection.
